english: Intelligence (al-'Aql) is the guide of a believer.
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book of Intelligence and Ignorance, Hadith 24

arabic: يَا عَلِيُّ لا فَقْرَ أَشَدُّ مِنَ الْجَهْلِ وَلا مَالَ أَعْوَدُ مِنَ الْعَقْلِ
english: Oh Ali, no poverty is more severe than ignorance and no wealth is more profitable than intelligence.
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book of Intelligence and Ignorance, Hadith 25

arabic: إِنَّمَا يُدَاقُّ اللَّهُ الْعِبَادَ فِي الْحِسَابِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ عَلَى قَدْرِ مَا آتَاهُمْ مِنَ الْعُقُولِ فِي الدُّنْيَا
english: On the Day of Judgment, Allah will hold everyone accountable according to the degree of intelligence He had given them in their worldly life.
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book of Intelligence and Ignorance, Hadith 7

arabic: الْعَقْلُ غِطَاءٌ سَتِيرٌ وَ الْفَضْلُ جَمَالٌ ظَاهِرٌ فَاسْتُرْ خَلَلَ خُلُقِكَ بِفَضْلِكَ وَ قَاتِلْ هَوَاكَ بِعَقْلِكَ تَسْلَمْ لَكَ الْمَوَدَّةُ وَ تَظْهَرْ لَكَ الْمَحَبَّةُ
english: Intelligence is a complete cover (protection) for one and extra knowledge and skills are evident beauty. Compensate your physical shortcomings with your extra knowledge and skills. Fight your desires with help from your intelligence and in so doing friendship is surrendered to and love becomes manifest for you.
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book of Intelligence and Ignorance, Hadith 13

arabic: الرَّغْبَةُ مِفْتَاحُ التَّعَبِ وَ مَطِيَّةُ النَّصَبِ
english: Greed motivates the hearts of the ignorant people, longings and yearning keep them as hostages and deceit clings to them.
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book of Intelligence and Ignorance, Hadith 16

english: The most perfect of people in intellect are the best of them in character.
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book of Intelligence and Ignorance, Hadith 17

arabic: إِذَا قَامَ قَائِمُنَا وَضَعَ اللَّهُ يَدَهُ عَلَى رُءُوسِ الْعِبَادِ فَجَمَعَ بِهَا عُقُولَهُمْ وَ كَمَلَتْ بِهِ أَحْلَامُهُمْ
english: When our Qa'im (Imam al-Mahdi) rises, Allah will place His hand over the heads of His servants and He will place their intelligence together and complete their understanding.
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book of Intelligence and Ignorance, Hadith 21

arabic: إِعْجَابُ الْمَرْءِ بِنَفْسِهِ دَلِيلٌ عَلَى ضَعْفِ عَقْلِهِ
english: Self glorification is an indication of the weakness of one's intelligence.
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book of Intelligence and Ignorance, Hadith 31

arabic: طَلَبُ الْعِلْمِ فَرِيضَةٌ عَلَى كُلِّ مُسْلِمٍ أَلَا وَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ بُغَاةَ الْعِلْمِ
english: Seeking knowledge is obligatory for every Muslim. Let it be known that Allah loves those who seek knowledge.
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book on Virtue of Knowledge, Chapter on the necessity to seek knowledge and the recommendations to learn, Hadith 1

arabic: تَفَقَّهُوا فِي دِينِ اللَّهِ وَ لَا تَكُونُوا أَعْرَاباً فَإِنَّهُ مَنْ لَمْ يَتَفَقَّهْ فِي دِينِ اللَّهِ لَمْ يَنْظُرِ اللَّهُ إِلَيْهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَ لَمْ يُزَكِّ لَهُ عَمَلًا
english: It is necessary for you to have a good understanding of the religion of Allah. Do not be like the Bedouins, because those who do not gain a good understanding of the religion, Allah will not look at them on the Day of Judgement, and none of their deeds will be cleansed.
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book on Virtue of Knowledge, Chapter on the necessity to seek knowledge and the recommendations to learn, Hadith 7

arabic: الْكَمَالُ كُلُّ الْكَمَالِ التَّفَقُّهُ فِي الدِّينِ وَ الصَّبْرُ عَلَى النَّائِبَةِ وَ تَقْدِيرُ الْمَعِيشَةِ
english: The most perfect of all perfection is a good understanding of religion, exercising patience during affliction and setting up of means of living.
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book on Virtue of Knowledge, Chapter on the quality of knowledge its virtue and the virtue of the scholars, Hadith 4

arabic: رَجُلٌ رَاوِيَةٌ لِحَدِيثِنَا يَبُثُّهُ فِي النَّاسِ وَ يُشَدِّدُ بِهِ قُلُوبَ شِيعَتِنَا أَفْضَلُ مِنْ أَلْفِ عَابِدٍ
english: I asked Abu ‘Abdillah (al-Sadiq) (as), 'There is a man who narrates your hadiths, spreads them among people, and ties them to their hearts and the hearts of your Shi'a. Also, there is perhaps a worshipper among your Shi'a who does not have your narrations. Which of the two is better?' He said, 'The one who narrates our hadiths and ties them to the hearts of our Shi'a is better than 1,000 worshippers.'
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book on Virtue of Knowledge, Chapter on the quality of knowledge its virtue and the virtue of the scholars, Hadith 9

arabic: مَنْ تَعَلَّمَ الْعِلْمَ وَ عَمِلَ بِهِ وَ عَلَّمَ لِلَّهِ دُعِيَ فِي مَلَكُوتِ السَّمَاوَاتِ عَظِيماً فَقِيلَ تَعَلَّمَ لِلَّهِ وَ عَمِلَ لِلَّهِ وَ عَلَّمَ لِلَّهِ
english: Whoever acquires knowledge and who practices what he has learned and teaches it to others for the sake of Allah, among the angels of heavens he will be called a great personality. It will be said there: ‘He learned for the sake of Allah, practiced for the sake of Allah and taught it for the sake of Allah.’
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book on Virtue of Knowledge, Chapter on the reward for the scholars and those who seek knowledge, Hadith 6

arabic: زَكَاةُ الْعِلْمِ أَنْ تُعَلِّمَهُ عِبَادَ اللَّهِ
english: Zakat, the tax, on knowledge is teaching it to the servants of Allah.
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book on Virtue of Knowledge, Chapter on Giving Knowledge as Charity, Hadith 3

arabic: أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِذَا عَلِمْتُمْ فَاعْمَلُوا بِمَا عَلِمْتُمْ...
english: O people, if you learn and gain knowledge you must act accordingly so that you may have guidance. A learned person who disregards his knowledge is like a lost and ignorant person who never comes to the right path due to his ignorance...
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book on Virtue of Knowledge, Chapter on Utilization of Knowledge, Hadith 6

arabic: مَنْهُومَانِ لَا يَشْبَعَانِ مَنْهُومٌ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَ مَنْهُومٌ فِي الْعِلْمِ...
english: Two kinds of people with great appetites do not become satisfied. A person of great appetite for the worldly gains and a person with great appetite for knowledge...
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book on Virtue of Knowledge, Chapter on Those who use their Knowledge to Fill their Stomach and are Boastful for it, Hadith 1

arabic: مَنْ أَرَادَ الْحَدِيثَ لِمَنْفَعَةِ الدُّنْيَا لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ نَصِيبٌ وَ مَنْ أَرَادَ بِهِ خَيْرَ الْآخِرَةِ أَعْطَاهُ اللَّهُ خَيْرَ الدُّنْيَا وَ الْآخِرَةِ
english: If someone wants Hadith for the worldly benefits he will have no share for it in the next life, but if one wants Hadith for the good of the next life, Allah will give him the good of both this and the next life.
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book on Virtue of Knowledge, Chapter on Those who use their Knowledge to Fill their Stomach and are Boastful for it, Hadith 2

arabic: مَنْ طَلَبَ الْعِلْمَ لِيُبَاهِيَ بِهِ الْعُلَمَاءَ أَوْ يُمَارِيَ بِهِ السُّفَهَاءَ أَوْ يَصْرِفَ بِهِ وُجُوهَ النَّاسِ إِلَيْهِ فَلْيَتَبَوَّأْ مَقْعَدَهُ مِنَ النَّارِ إِنَّ الرِّئَاسَةَ لَا تَصْلُحُ إِلَّا لِأَهْلِهَا
english: He who seeks knowledge to boast among scholars, engage in disputes with the foolish, or attract people's attention to himself, then let him prepare a seat for himself in the Hellfire. Indeed, leadership is not suitable except for those who deserve it.
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book on Virtue of Knowledge, Chapter on Those who use their Knowledge to Fill their Stomach and are Boastful for it, Hadith 6

arabic: رَوِّحُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ بِطَرَائِفِ الْحِكْمَةِ فَإِنَّهَا تَكِلُّ كَمَا تَكِلُّ الْأَبْدَانُ
english: Relax your souls with new sparks of wisdom, because souls also tire as bodies do.
book: al-Kafi, Vol 1, The Book on Virtue of Knowledge, Chapter on Miscellaneous Issues, Hadith 1
